title: FAIR Analysis
subtitle: Factor Analysis of Information Risk — Monte Carlo Quantification
notEnabledDesc: Your organization is currently using qualitative risk assessment (5x5 matrix). FAIR quantification provides financial risk values in EUR.
enableInSettings: An administrator can enable FAIR or Hybrid methodology in the organization settings.
lefDesc: How often does this loss event occur per year? Use PERT distribution: minimum, most likely, and maximum estimates.
lmDesc: How much does a single loss event cost in EUR? Provide minimum, most likely, and maximum estimates.
lossComponentsDesc: Distribution of total loss across FAIR taxonomy categories. Must sum to 100%.
exceedanceDesc: Probability that the annualized loss exceeds a given threshold value.
sensitivityDesc: Shows which input parameter has the greatest impact on the ALE result.
p5Desc: 5% of cases result in losses below this value
p95Desc: Value at Risk — only 5% of scenarios exceed this
noResultsDesc: Run a Monte Carlo simulation first to see the results here.
compareSubtitle: Side-by-side comparison of up to 5 quantified risks
compareEmptyDesc: Select up to 5 risks to compare their FAIR quantification results.
portfolioSubtitle: Executive view: Total cyber risk exposure in EUR
riskScatterDesc: X-axis: relative frequency, Y-axis: tail risk (P95 - P50), Bubble size: ALE P50
topRisksTitle: Top 10 Risks by Annual Loss Expectancy
topRisksSubtitle: Ranked by ALE P50 (Median) — financial impact in EUR
noQuantifiedRisks: No risks have been quantified with FAIR yet. Configure FAIR parameters and run a simulation for individual risks.
methodologyDesc: Choose how risks are assessed in your organization.
qualitativeDesc: Traditional likelihood x impact scoring on a 1-5 scale.
fairDesc: All risks quantified in EUR using Monte Carlo simulation.
hybridDesc: Qualitative by default, FAIR optional for high-scoring risks (score >= 15).
